notes: Zero-byte file with .pdf extension. The magic-byte sniff finds nothing; ingest worker rejects with ingest.empty_file.
Zero-byte file with .docx extension. Same handling as empty.pdf.
PDF header + stream object that declares more bytes than the file contains. Parser hits EOF mid-stream.
Valid DOCX zip body wrapped in a .pdf filename. Magic-byte sniff sees PK\x03\x04, not %PDF. Trusting the extension would route this to the PDF parser and crash it.
Valid zip envelope; word/document.xml has an unclosed <w:t> tag. Parser should raise a clear parse error rather than silently truncating.
Valid zip with [Content_Types].xml and _rels/.rels but no word/document.xml. OOXML requires this part; absence is a hard parse error.

DOCX zip whose directory contains an entry named '../../../etc/wikore-zipslip-canary'. A naive extractor that joins entry names with the destination dir would write outside the sandbox. The ingest worker MUST normalize each entry path and reject any that escape the destination.

expected_behaviour: refuse to resolve external entities; ingest stripped text; audit
notes: DOCX whose document.xml declares an external entity pointing at file:///etc/passwd. XML parsers configured without secure-by-default settings will resolve it. wikore-ingest's XML parser MUST disable external entity resolution; an explicit security audit event SHOULD be logged when an external entity is encountered.
DOCX with 2000 levels of nested elements. Not a billion-laughs (XXE hardening already blocks entity-based bombs); exercises raw element-depth limits.
XLSX with one XML attribute holding 1 MB of data. Defends against parsers that allocate per-attribute buffers without an upper bound.

expected_behaviour: AV scan -> reject; audit; security event
notes: The standard EICAR antivirus test signature. 68-byte ASCII string designed by EICAR specifically so AV products can be tested without distributing real malware. NOT a virus; AV products are required to flag this exact byte sequence.

expected_behaviour: AV scan -> reject; audit; security event
notes: Minimal PDF carrying the EICAR signature in a content stream. Demonstrates that AV scanning must inspect the full byte sequence, not just filename or MIME.

expected_behaviour: detect /JS or /JavaScript; strip from extraction; ingest text; audit
notes: PDF with an /OpenAction containing /JS. Some PDF viewers execute this; ingest workers should detect, strip, and audit. Text content can still be extracted and embedded.

expected_behaviour: reject OR strip vbaProject.bin + ingest cleaned doc; audit
notes: DOCX whose zip directory contains word/vbaProject.bin. The bin bytes here are decoy (not a real VBA project) but the entry's presence is the canonical macro-bearing signal. Decision (reject vs strip-and-ingest) is per-tenant policy in companies.settings.

expected_behaviour: ingest normally; mitigation is at LLM layer, not ingest
notes: Plain-text injection attempt. Ingest behaviour: TREAT AS NORMAL EVIDENCE. The mitigation is twofold: (1) system prompt MUST framing-isolate retrieved evidence from user instructions; (2) EvidenceGate prevents the retriever from feeding chunks that the user is not entitled to see. The eval harness exercises both.

expected_behaviour: strip HTML during markdown parse; ingest cleaned text only
notes: Injection text hidden inside a <span style='display:none'>. The chunk's embedded text MUST NOT contain the hidden payload. Markdown parser should treat HTML tags as text (raw passthrough is unsafe) or strip them.

expected_behaviour: strip Cf-category Unicode (esp. U+E0000..U+E007F) during normalization
notes: Injection encoded in the Unicode Tags block (U+E0000..U+E007F). Invisible to humans, opaque to most renderers, but survives text extraction. Text normalization MUST strip these codepoints before embedding so the LLM never sees them.

expected_behaviour: ingest text; do not auto-fetch external image URLs; UI requires explicit click
notes: Markdown image pointing at an attacker URL with templated query parameters. Auto-fetching during embedding or rendering would leak the document's existence and tenant IP. UI rendering policy: external images require user click; cite-only mode never resolves them.
